food_subgroup: Whole wheat flatbread stuffed with spiced potato

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Aloo Paratha. Nearest parent is a stuffed flatbread. A new concept for Indian stuffed paratha preparations is recommended.
ontology.foodex2_description: Stuffed whole wheat flatbread (paratha) with potato filling — closest parent match; no exact FoodEx2 code exists for Aloo Paratha.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for Aloo Paratha. A01GP used as closest parent for stuffed flatbread category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept identified for Aloo Paratha as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard piece of Aloo Paratha with ghee weighs approximately 130 g, which is the typical serving size at North Indian restaurants and home kitchens.

nutrient_source.title: Aloo Paratha — HPB Singapore Foodo Database, Food Code F05020001; cross-referenced with ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB), Singapore; Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo Database (Food Code F05020001) with cross-reference to ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables for micronutrient validation. Both are approved primary sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Whole Wheat Paratha with Potato Filling — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for whole wheat flatbread and potato-based preparations
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Aloo Paratha exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 66 (medium) is estimated from the combination of whole wheat paratha (GI ~62) and potato filling (GI ~78), moderated by the fat content of ghee which slows gastric emptying. Glycemic load of 34.7 calculated per 130 g serving: (66 × 40.5 × 1.30) / 100 = 34.7 g. This estimation is consistent with published GI values for similar mixed wheat-potato preparations.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 406 mg per 130 g serving; saturated fat from ghee contributes to cardiovascular load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Aloo Paratha to one piece per meal, avoid additional salt or high-sodium accompaniments such as pickle, and choose low-fat yogurt as a side to reduce overall sodium and saturated fat intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (66) combined with high glycemic load (~35 per piece) due to the potato filling and whole wheat dough; total carbohydrate per serving approximately 52.7 g
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit intake to half a piece (65 g) per meal, pair with a high-protein side such as plain yogurt or dal to blunt the glycaemic response, and monitor post-meal blood glucose, as the glycaemic load per standard serving is substantial.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 4.5 g per 100 g (5.85 g per 130 g serving) from ghee; cholesterol 18 mg per 100 g; whole wheat fibre (3.8 g per 100 g) provides partial cardioprotective benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Aloo Paratha in moderation, preferably cooked with minimal ghee or substituting with a small amount of unsaturated oil, and pair with vegetables or legumes to offset the saturated fat load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Aloo Paratha is the quintessential Punjabi breakfast and comfort food — a generous whole wheat flatbread (atta) stuffed with a spiced mashed potato filling seasoned with cumin, green chilli, fresh coriander, amchur (dried mango powder), and garam masala, then cooked on a hot tawa (griddle) with ghee until golden and slightly crispy on both sides. Each piece weighs approximately 130 g and provides 354 kcal, 8.1 g protein, 13.7 g fat, and 52.7 g carbohydrate. The glycaemic index is approximately 66 (medium) but the glycaemic load per piece is high (~35), making portion control important for diabetic individuals. The ghee contributes saturated fat (5.85 g per piece) and a rich flavour, while the whole wheat dough provides dietary fibre (4.9 g per piece), B vitamins, and selenium. Traditionally served with cold fresh yogurt (dahi) and tangy pickle (achar). In Singapore, Aloo Paratha is available at North Indian restaurants and some hawker stalls. It is suitable for vegetarians and halal diets. Clinically, it is a high-carbohydrate, moderate-fat dish that requires portion awareness for individuals managing diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease.
common_user_queries: how many calories in one aloo paratha

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo Database (Food Code F05020001), an approved primary source for Singapore food records, with cross-reference to ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables. The food identity is exact. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database, reducing GI confidence to medium, but all other data points are from approved primary sources.
notes: vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo Database (Food Code F05020001) — Aloo Paratha contains no animal-derived dairy or meat in the standard preparation; ghee contributes negligible B12 at this serving size, confirmed zero in the source. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo Database. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Aloo Paratha not reported in HPB Foodo Database or ICMR-NIN tables as of 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this preparation not reported in HPB Foodo Database or ICMR-NIN tables as of 2026. GI value of 66 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on whole wheat paratha and potato filling components; no direct GI measurement for Aloo Paratha is published in the database. Glycemic load of 34.7 calculated per 130 g default serving. Portions in the input contained three duplicate entries for '1 serving (150g)'; these have been deduplicated to a single secondary portion. copper_mg (0.15) and manganese_mg (0.8) from input per_100g are not mapped to the RESULT template per_100g schema but are preserved in notes: copper 0.15 mg/100g, manganese 0.8 mg/100g (source: enriched_json, confidence 0.9). vitamin_k_mcg (2.5 mg/100g) similarly not in template schema; preserved in notes (source: enriched_json, confidence 0.9).
